qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public Chat Trigger n8nUserAuth mode accepts any n8n-auth cookie
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about Merge node SQL Query mode enabling arbitrary file write/RCE, while this finding is a Chat Trigger webhook cookie-validation bypass in a different component and code path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's Merge node SQL Query arbitrary-file-write root cause. The only manifest-backed qualifying finding is a Chat Trigger authentication bypass in a different component and code path.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe an authenticated-user issue in the Merge node's SQL Query mode that allows arbitrary file write on the n8n server and can lead to RCE.

chat_trigger_n8n_user_auth_bypass points to Chat Trigger webhook authentication logic in packages/@n8n/nodes-langchain and live webhook handling, not Merge node SQL processing or filesystem write behavior.
The run manifest lists only one qualifying finding folder, and it is the Chat Trigger finding rather than any Merge-node or SQL Query finding.
A session-wide search across the manifest-backed finding folders found no qualifying report text referencing Merge node SQL Query handling or arbitrary file-write behavior.
